subject: grammar
title: Non-defining Relative Clauses
instruction: Rewrite the sentences below as single sentences using non-defining relative clauses (who, which, whose, where). Include commas where necessary.
passage:
Example: My father lives in a small house full of ornaments. This makes it really difficult to clean. 
Answer: My father lives in a small house full of ornaments, which makes it really difficult to clean.

questions.text: Some students take a year out before university. This allows them to work or travel.
answers: Some students take a year out before university, which allows them to work or travel.
some students take a year out before university, which allows them to work or travel.
Some students take a year out before university, which allows them to work or travel.
some students take a year out before university, which allows them to work or travel.

questions.text: The Guggenheim Museum is in Bilbao. It only displays contemporary art.
answers: The Guggenheim Museum, which is in Bilbao, only displays contemporary art.
the guggenheim museum, which is in bilbao, only displays contemporary art.
The Guggenheim Museum, which is in Bilbao, only displays contemporary art.
the guggenheim museum, which is in bilbao, only displays contemporary art.

questions.text: My English teacher is leaving. His lectures are very interesting.
answers: My English teacher, whose lectures are very interesting, is leaving.
my english teacher, whose lectures are very interesting, is leaving.
My English teacher, whose lectures are very interesting, is leaving.
my english teacher, whose lectures are very interesting, is leaving.

questions.text: The lecture was about current economic policy. It was not very easy to understand.
answers: The lecture, which was about current economic policy, was not very easy to understand.
the lecture, which was about current economic policy, was not very easy to understand.
The lecture, which was about current economic policy, was not very easy to understand.
the lecture, which was about current economic policy, was not very easy to understand.

questions.text: In 1951 my parents arrived in New York. They stayed there for the rest of their lives.
answers: My parents arrived in New York in 1951, where they stayed for the rest of their lives.
my parents arrived in new york in 1951, where they stayed for the rest of their lives.
My parents arrived in New York in 1951, where they stayed for the rest of their lives.
my parents arrived in new york in 1951, where they stayed for the rest of their lives.

questions.text: I gave my assignment to the faculty secretary. She was not very friendly.
answers: I gave my assignment to the faculty secretary, who was not very friendly.
i gave my assignment to the faculty secretary, who was not very friendly.
I gave my assignment to the faculty secretary, who was not very friendly.
i gave my assignment to the faculty secretary, who was not very friendly.
